Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix missing .js extension in airInc service import

Add .js extension to shared/schema import in server/services/airInc.ts.
Remove temporary rebuild comment from storage.ts.

This was the missing piece causing ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Derive win probability from margin, drop separate classifier

Replace the 3-model architecture (win classifier + margin regressor + total
regressor) with 2 models (margin + total) plus a logistic function that
derives win probability from predicted margin. This ensures margin and win
probability can never disagree.

- Train margin and total regressors in parallel via ThreadPoolExecutor
- Derive logistic k analytically from margin std (k = pi / (std * sqrt(3)))
- Training time: ~40 min -> ~13 min (fewer models + parallelism)
- Margin/total accuracy unchanged; win probs now better calibrated

commit.message:
fix: live badge overridden by YouTube API returning same video as both live and upcoming

When a stream goes live, YouTube's API returns the same video ID in both
eventType=live and eventType=upcoming searches. This caused upcomingVideoId
to take priority over liveVideoId, showing "Upcoming" instead of "Live Now".

- fetch-videos.js: clear upcomingVideoId when liveVideoId is already set
- index.html: make isLive take priority over isUpcoming in badge logic
- videos.json: clear stale upcomingVideoId for current live stream

commit.message:
Fix UTF-8 chunker panic on multi-byte character boundaries (#29)

The overlap slicer used saturating_sub which could land inside a
multi-byte UTF-8 character, causing a panic on slice. Now snaps
backward to the nearest valid char boundary. Adds regression tests
for box-drawing characters and emoji.

Multi-page HTML report with recursive crawling from entry

Instead of generating a single report.html, specdown now produces a
multi-page static site with shared style.css/script.js and per-document
HTML pages. The entry file is a regular markdown page that serves as the
starting point for BFS recursive link crawling — all linked .md and
.spec.md files are discovered transitively. Markdown links between pages
are rewritten from .md/.spec.md to .html in the output.

Add comprehensive localStorage protection for free users

CRITICAL FIX: Prevents data loss for free tier users

Changes:
1. **Complete Export/Import** - Now exports ALL data fields
   - Added: accounts, journalEntries, goals, riskRules, onboarding
   - Tracks last backup date
   - Shows item count on import

2. **Storage Usage Display** - Shows localStorage usage in Settings
   - Visual progress bar (green/red based on usage)
   - Warns at 80%: "Storage almost full!"
   - Displays: "2.3 MB / ~10 MB"

3. **Backup Reminders** - For free users only
   - Shows "Last backup: X days ago" in Settings
   - Warns if >30 days since backup
   - "Backup Now" quick action button

4. **Data Warning Banner** - Dashboard banner for free users
   - Warns data is stored locally only
   - Explains risks (incognito, cache clear, different devices)
   - CTA to export backups or upgrade to Pro
   - Dismissible

5. **Incognito Mode Detection** - Warns users immediately
   - Detects private/incognito browsing
   - Toast warning: "Data will be lost when you close window"
   - Suggests normal browsing or Pro upgrade

6. **Pro Upsell** - Smart CTAs in Settings
   - "Want automatic cloud backup? Upgrade to Pro"
   - Only shown to free users

Impact:
- Prevents data loss from cache clearing
- Prevents data loss from incognito mode
- Prevents data loss from storage quota limits
- Educates users about local-only storage
- Encourages regular backups
- Drives Pro upgrades

company.enabledFeatures was empty [] because featureSync.ts only handled

commit.message:
Revise AI generation plan: add Stage 6.5, redesign Stage 7

Stage 6.5 (Unified Content Refactor): all dungeon content uses the same
library system with a permanent flag. 4 precreated monsters, 7 tiles,
64 rooms loaded as permanent entries. AI prompt sees a flat list — no
built-in vs custom distinction. Sprites sent via custom registries.

Stage 7 revised: ~50% precreated + ~50% custom rooms, lazy generation,
library capacities 96/8/14, separate persistence for permanent (git)
vs custom (gitignored data/).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Layers 1-2: Core macros and runtime library

Layer 1 - Syntax macros (~350 lines):
- def: simple bindings, functions, optional args, rest args
- def*: case-lambda shorthand
- defstruct: maps to native Chez define-record-type with accessors/mutators
- defclass: single inheritance via parent records
- defmethod: method dispatch via bind-method! + record-type-descriptor
- defrule/defrules: syntax-rules macro definition
- match: full pattern matching (variables, literals, lists, pairs, cons,
  predicates, and/or/not combinators, wildcard, else)
- try/catch/finally: maps to guard + dynamic-wind
- while/until: loop macros
- hash-literal/hash-eq-literal: hash table constructors
- let-hash: hash destructuring with .x/.?x/.$x accessors

Layer 2 - Runtime library (~250 lines):
- Method dispatch: ~ operator, bind-method!, call-method with inheritance
- Hash tables: full Gerbil API on native Chez hashtables
- Keywords: #:name tagged symbols
- Error utilities: error-message, error-irritants
- Utilities: displayln, iota (with start/step), 1+, 1-

68 tests passing (core) + 65 tests (reader) = 133 total.

commit.message:
Implement P0 pentest fixes: block 6 more bypass vectors

- Recursive base64 decoding (catches double/triple encoding)
- Block text-transform-to-shell (rev|sh, tr|sh, sed|sh, awk|sh)
- Block interpreter+obfuscation combos (python/ruby/node/perl with
  base64, chr, decode, exec, system calls)
- 2 new hardcore rules: transform-pipe-to-shell, interpreter-obfuscation
- 8 new attack simulation tests for pentest round 2 bypasses
- 3 new unit tests for detection functions
- Total: 103 tests (52 unit + 36 attack + 15 rollback), all passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Build investor portal backend (Phase 1: Database & Logic)

INVESTOR PORTAL FOUNDATION COMPLETE 🚀

DATABASE (8 tables):
✅ investors - Accounts, verification, financials
✅ investment_pools - Shared capital pools with profit terms
✅ investor_pool_shares - Ownership tracking
✅ investor_capital - Deposits/withdrawals
✅ investor_vehicles - Vehicles funded by pools
✅ investor_distributions - Profit payouts
✅ investor_reports - Monthly statements
✅ pool_transactions - Complete audit log (Plaid-ready)

SQL FUNCTIONS (6):
✅ deploy_capital_to_vehicle() - Links vehicle to pool
✅ process_investor_deposit() - Handles deposits
✅ increment_investor_profit() - Updates totals
✅ recalculate_pool_ownership() - Recalcs percentages
✅ process_distribution_payout() - Marks paid
✅ get_investor_dashboard_stats() - Dashboard metrics

EDGE FUNCTIONS (2):
✅ deploy-capital-to-vehicle - Deploy funds to purchase
✅ distribute-vehicle-profit - Calculate & distribute profit

BUSINESS MODEL:
- Investors deposit capital → Pool buys vehicles
- Vehicle sells → Profit split (60% investor, 20% platform, 20% dealer)
- Automated distribution to each investor based on ownership %
- Capital returns to pool for next purchase

EXAMPLE FLOW:
1. 3 investors deposit $100k total
2. Deploy $18.5k to buy Toyota RAV4
3. Sells for $24.5k (45 days, $6k profit)
4. Auto-distribute: $3,600 to investors, $1,200 platform, $1,200 dealer
5. Each investor gets % of $3,600 based on ownership
6. Capital returns to pool, ready to deploy again

SECURITY:
✅ RLS policies - Investors see only their data
✅ Encrypted Plaid tokens
✅ Complete audit trail

NEXT PHASE:
- Dashboard UI (real-time charts, portfolio view)
- Plaid integration (bank linking, ACH transfers)
- Admin panel (manage rates, approve investors)

feat: upgrade embedding model to BGE-small-en-v1.5, improve LLM quality

  embeddings use BGE prefix, chunk embeddings don't; cache dir: fastembed-bge/

  bge-small-en-v1.5; model version persisted to embed_model.json

- max_new_tokens: 800 → 1024; MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS: 5500 → 7000

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(jenkins): Add githubPush trigger and align with ReasonBridge patterns

Root cause: Jenkins was not triggering builds on PRs because:
1. Missing `triggers { githubPush() }` block in pipeline
2. Overly restrictive `headRegexFilter` blocking new branch discovery
3. Missing helper functions for PR detection

Changes:
- Add `triggers { githubPush() }` to respond to GitHub webhooks
- Add `isFullCI()` and `checkForOpenPR()` helper functions
- Remove restrictive headRegexFilter from job config
- Add gitHubSkipNotifications() for explicit status control
- Update branch discovery to match ReasonBridge patterns
- Add flakyTestQuarantine.groovy for flaky test detection
- Add scripts/delete-zombie-jobs.sh utility

This aligns KinDash Jenkins configuration with the working
ReasonBridge setup.

Fix proxy 502: forward only essential headers to Jira

Browser sends accept-encoding, sec-fetch-* etc. that confuse Jira.
Now only forwards Authorization, Accept, Content-Type headers.
Also filter response headers to prevent transfer-encoding conflicts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: robust hotplug, expanded decoder probe, OSD shows output resolution

Hotplug:
- auto_recovery_thread Phase 1 now scans all pipeline_mgr.displays (all
  DRM connectors) independently of cfg["displays"], so newly connected
  monitors are detected even when config is empty
- Newly hotplugged display gets default config entry, splash, and NDI
  receiver advertisement automatically
- Phase 2 skips disconnected displays; reconnect is fully handled in Phase 1

Hardware decoder:
- _probe_mpp_decoder replaced by _probe_hw_decoder with full priority chain:
  Rockchip MPP → VA-API (gst-va) → Legacy VA-API (gst-vaapi) →
  V4L2 stateless → V4L2 M2M → avdec_h264 (software fallback)
- Board detection now board-agnostic; any Linux SBC or x86 gets best decoder
- decoder_chain stored for future NDI|HX compressed-stream support

OSD:
- Text changed from "source | ip:8080" to "WxH  fps" (output resolution
  and configured framerate) — matches what is actually being output
- Same font/style as splash text (Sans Bold 11, top-right corner)
